title: The Coup

jokes.text: The only cure I know for the Monday blues is Varsity Blues.
jokes.setup: Someone says they're hungry on Monday morning
jokes.punchline: Michael connects 'Monday blues' to the football movie 'Varsity Blues' as a solution
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael wordplay with his particular brand of confident nonsense. Good character integration showing his tendency to solve problems with entertainment.

jokes.text: with the exception of an episode of Entourage, which Michael made us watch six times.
jokes.setup: Pam explaining Movie Monday's evolution through training videos to movies
jokes.punchline: The absurd detail that they watched one Entourage episode six times
jokes.explanation: Excellent character detail that's both specific and absurd. Perfect Michael obsession that feels completely authentic to his character.

jokes.text: Kevin? Would you like to do the scenes from last week? / Why him?
jokes.setup: Michael asks Kevin to recap previous movie scenes
jokes.punchline: Someone immediately questions this choice, implying Kevin is not the ideal person
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing office dynamics and Kevin's reputation. Economy of two short lines creates the joke.

jokes.text: Not everyone approves of Movie Monday. I won't say who. / I don't approve. I don't.
jokes.setup: Someone coyly says they won't reveal who disapproves
jokes.punchline: Immediate cut to Angela bluntly stating her disapproval
jokes.explanation: Classic mockumentary structure with perfect timing. The juxtaposition creates the humor through dramatic irony.

jokes.text: How would a movie increase productivity, Michael? / People work faster after. / Magically? / No. They have to, to make up for the time they lost watching the movie.
jokes.setup: Jan asks how movies could increase productivity
jokes.punchline: Michael's circular logic that people work faster to compensate for time lost
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael logic - technically sound but completely missing the point. Jan's 'Magically?' adds perfect comic timing.

jokes.text: Do I need to hire a babysitter for you, Michael? / Kitchen. Some little 14-year old girl whose job it is, is to limit...
jokes.setup: Jan rhetorically asks if Michael needs a babysitter
jokes.punchline: Michael takes it literally and starts elaborating on the babysitter scenario
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael move of taking rhetorical questions literally and making them worse. Good cringe factor.

jokes.text: Reject a woman, and she will never let it go. One of the many defects of their kind. Also weak arms.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining Jan's behavior toward Michael
jokes.punchline: Casually adds 'weak arms' as another female 'defect'
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight - starts with pseudo-wisdom then adds completely random physical observation. Perfect character voice and unexpected escalation.

jokes.text: but sometimes I just think you need to grow a pair.
jokes.setup: Angela lecturing Dwight about patience and loyalty
jokes.punchline: Prim Angela using crude expression 'grow a pair'
jokes.explanation: Funny contrast between Angela's usual propriety and the crude expression. Good character moment.

jokes.text: Pam and I would sometimes hum the same high-pitched note and try to get Dwight to make an appointment with an ear doctor. And Pam called it Pretendonitis.
jokes.setup: Jim describing pranks they used to pull instead of playing video games
jokes.punchline: The made-up word 'Pretendonitis' combining pretend and a medical condition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that's both clever and perfectly fits Jim and Pam's prank style. The fake medical condition name is inspired.

jokes.text: It's just, I felt like it was time to maybe, just get new clothes.
jokes.setup: Pam trying to explain why she bought new clothes
jokes.punchline: Her inability to articulate a simple reason, trailing off awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Subtle character moment showing Pam's difficulty expressing personal changes. The awkward delivery sells the joke.

jokes.text: Fashion show! Fashion show! Fashion show at lunch!
jokes.setup: Kelly getting excited about Pam's new clothes
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly insistent chanting about having a fashion show
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kelly energy with the repetitive chanting. Good escalation and character integration.

jokes.text: I'm just killing Germans any way I can. / We're on the German team. / Shoot the British.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining his video game strategy
jokes.punchline: Revelation that he's been shooting his own team members
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff showing Jim's video game incompetence. The misunderstanding is earned and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Pull over at Exit 40. There is a Liz Claiborne outlet. I know you like that store.
jokes.setup: Dwight arranging a secret meeting with Jan
jokes.punchline: His knowledge of Jan's shopping preferences as a meeting location suggestion
jokes.explanation: Creepy and funny how Dwight knows Jan's shopping habits. Good setup for the escalating surveillance revelation.

jokes.text: Many of your blouses are Claibornes. / How do you know that? / It's part of my job. / No, it's not. It's officially not.
jokes.setup: Jan questioning how Dwight knows her clothing brands
jokes.punchline: Dwight claiming it's his job and Jan's firm correction that it's 'officially not'
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Dwight's creepy surveillance behavior. Jan's 'officially not' is a great punchline delivery.

jokes.text: I have to have an emergency crown put in. / Yes. / It's a new dentist. / He's far. I might be gone three hours. / Three hours? Wow! Have fun.
jokes.setup: Dwight elaborately lying about going to the dentist
jokes.punchline: Jim's enthusiastic 'Have fun!' response to dental work
jokes.explanation: Jim's cheerful response to what should be an unpleasant experience. Good character moment showing his suspicion.

jokes.text: New blouse? Halter-top? Camisole? Teddy?
jokes.setup: Jim asking what Pam bought while shopping
jokes.punchline: The progression from normal clothes to increasingly intimate items
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from innocent to suggestive clothing items. Shows Jim's flirtatious side.

jokes.text: And I could care less about my co-workers.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his motivations to Jan
jokes.punchline: His blunt admission of not caring about coworkers
jokes.explanation: Perfectly cold Dwight moment. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Oh, by the way, there's a new Ann Taylor outlet store near here. I know you like their earrings.
jokes.setup: Dwight having just pitched himself for manager
jokes.punchline: Continuing to demonstrate his creepy knowledge of Jan's shopping habits
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the earlier creepy shopping knowledge. The casual delivery after a serious business pitch is perfect.

jokes.text: She's my ex-lover-ish.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why he thought Dwight couldn't turn Jan against him
jokes.punchline: The awkward qualifier 'ish' added to 'ex-lover'
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael attempt to make their relationship sound more significant than it was. The 'ish' qualifier is perfectly awkward.

jokes.text: It's too much. / What? I'm gonna return it. / No. You have to keep it today, just see how you feel.
jokes.setup: Pam feeling self-conscious about her new outfit
jokes.punchline: Jim encouraging her to keep wearing it, showing his interest
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Jim's support and interest. More touching than purely comedic.

jokes.text: It's the new guy. / Oh, I'm sorry, I don't know what we're talking about. / See what I mean?
jokes.setup: Andy blaming their video game losses on Jim
jokes.punchline: Jim's innocent confusion followed by Andy using it as evidence
jokes.explanation: Good setup where Jim's honest confusion proves Andy's point. Well-structured comedic moment.

jokes.text: Andy, it's not... / I'm gonna kill you for real. / This game, the game is over. I'm really going to shoot you.
jokes.setup: Jim trying to explain video game strategy
jokes.punchline: Andy's escalation from game threats to real violence threats
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andy's intensity and poor impulse control. The escalation works well.

jokes.text: Your dentist's name is Crentist? / Yeah. / Sounds a lot like dentist. / Maybe that's why he became a dentist.
jokes.setup: Dwight claiming his dentist is named 'Crentist'
jokes.punchline: Dwight's logical explanation for the coincidental name
jokes.explanation: One of the best Office jokes ever. Perfect setup/payoff with Dwight's completely logical but absurd reasoning. Became a cultural touchstone.

jokes.text: Business is like a jungle. And I am like a tiger. And Dwight is like a monkey that stabs the tiger in the back with a stick.
jokes.setup: Michael creating a business metaphor with animals
jokes.punchline: The increasingly specific and ridiculous monkey betrayal scenario
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael metaphor that starts reasonable then gets absurdly specific. Great escalation and character voice.

jokes.text: Pun.
jokes.setup: Michael mentions transferring Dwight to another 'branch'
jokes.punchline: Michael proudly announcing his accidental pun
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - proud of his accidental wordplay. Single word punchline with maximum impact.

jokes.text: We don't have the technology.
jokes.setup: Michael saying there's no way to know what goes on in a tiger's head
jokes.punchline: The serious delivery of 'we don't have the technology'
jokes.explanation: Great callback to the animal metaphor with Michael treating it as literal science. Absurdist humor delivered straight.

jokes.text: She demoted me. / No. / Yeah. / You know what the craziest part of this is? She demoted me to your job.
jokes.setup: Michael telling Dwight he got demoted
jokes.punchline: The revelation that he was demoted specifically to Dwight's position
jokes.explanation: Good ironic twist - Michael's 'punishment' is Dwight's current job. Well-structured reveal.

jokes.text: I can't imagine this place without you. / Can't you? That's so nice.
jokes.setup: Dwight saying he can't imagine the office without Michael
jokes.punchline: Michael's sincere response to what should be obvious sarcasm
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael missing Dwight's sarcasm. The obliviousness is perfectly Michael.

jokes.text: Because Dwight never lies. / How does that qualify him to run a branch? / Because that's all it takes.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why Dwight should be manager
jokes.punchline: His reductive logic that not lying is the only qualification needed
jokes.explanation: Good Michael logic - oversimplifying complex management to one trait. Pam's question sets up the absurd response well.

jokes.text: I intend to lead you into the black with ferocity!
jokes.setup: Dwight giving his first speech as manager
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top militant approach to profitability
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight manager voice - treating business like warfare. 'With ferocity' is a great character touch.

jokes.text: I have this little old vacuum cleaner that's broken. If Dwight doesn't work out, maybe that could be manager.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Michael's prospects
jokes.punchline: Oscar's deadpan suggestion that a broken vacuum could manage better than Dwight
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan insult with specific absurd detail. The broken vacuum being better than Dwight is a perfect burn.

jokes.text: But you can be in charge of the women.
jokes.setup: Angela asking if she'll be working alone under Dwight
jokes.punchline: Dwight's casually sexist delegation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Dwight's outdated management style. Character-appropriate but uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Psychopath.
jokes.setup: Jim apparently doing something in the video game
jokes.punchline: Andy's single-word assessment of Jim's actions
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat with good delivery. Andy's intensity comes through in one word.

jokes.text: But you said you liked it. You've always admired it. / But that was before. I'm thinking about getting something German, something with decent gas mileage. Plus, that convertible is a ridiculous choice for this climate.
jokes.setup: Michael offering Dwight the Sebring he used to admire
jokes.punchline: Dwight's complete reversal now that he has power, with practical criticisms
jokes.explanation: Great power dynamic shift - Dwight rejecting what he once coveted. The practical reasons make it funnier.

jokes.text: Please? Please? / I don't know if I can trust you anymore. / You can't. You can't, but I promise I'll never betray you again.
jokes.setup: Dwight begging Michael not to fire him
jokes.punchline: Dwight agreeing he can't be trusted while simultaneously asking for trust
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight logic - admitting untrustworthiness while asking for another chance. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: And you can hug it out, bitch.
jokes.setup: Michael telling Dwight to get up after his groveling
jokes.punchline: The casual use of 'bitch' in the context of male bonding
jokes.explanation: Iconic Michael line that became widely quoted. Perfect blend of masculine posturing and casual vulgarity.

jokes.text: That is what men say to each other after a fight. They hug it out. And in doing so, they just let it go. Not a good idea to say that to a woman, however, I have found. Doesn't translate.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining the 'hug it out, bitch' philosophy
jokes.punchline: His warning about not using this approach with women
jokes.explanation: Good callback expanding on the previous joke. Michael's pseudo-wisdom about gender differences is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: But it turns out I was still a little angry and so I felt I needed to punish him just a little bit more. And I'm making him do my laundry for a year.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining that they hugged it out
jokes.punchline: His admission that he still wanted revenge and the laundry punishment
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Dwight's desperate offer. Shows Michael's pettiness despite the reconciliation.

tmdb_overview: Michael had made it a point to have Movie Monday every week. He called the office into the conference room to watch a 30-minute installment of Varsity Blues, but Jan made an unexpected visit and did not like what she saw.
